name: uniprot sentinel-classification + atlas registry axes-as-number — Ω-cycle 2-fix bundle
origin: hive raw 73 admissibility + raw 66 ai-native-error preservation; follow-up to 2026-04-26_bridge_health_check.md F7 + prior orphan backfill cycle
scope: two minimal surgical patches: (1) uniprot_bridge.hexa ok-decision branch when fallback engages; (2) atlas_witness_registry.sh axes_surfaced jq filter to handle scalar number form
constraints_honored: raw 73 admissibility — minimal patch, no scope creep
raw 66 ai-native-error — preserved error trailers (no error-message contract changes)
Bash 3.2 portable — registry tool fix uses jq filter only, no bashisms added

axes_surfaced.lines_changed: 319-330 (block expanded by 5 lines: 4 comment + 1 fails reset)
axes_surfaced.diff_summary: in fallback branch, after `function_brief = _str(row[5])` and before `} else { ... no_fallback ...} `, added `fails = []` with raw-73-justified comment. Mirrors gaia_bridge pattern where fallback success is a legitimate ok=true path.
axes_surfaced.before_state: fails accumulated `fetch_empty_or_short` (or `live_parse_partial`) from the fetch attempt; even after fallback fully populated all 5 required fields, len(fails)>0 → ok=false → sentinel FAIL with `all_ok=false`
axes_surfaced.after_state: fallback success clears prior fetch-attempt fails; field-validation checks at lines 332-335 still run and would push genuine data fails (uniprot_id_empty, etc.) if fallback row was malformed; ok=true only when fallback data is genuinely complete
edge_cases_preserved: live success path — source==live, fallback block skipped, fails empty if all live fields populate
live partial parse — source stays fallback (line 312), fails gets `live_parse_partial` then cleared, fallback table consulted
fallback-missing-accession — `no_fallback_for_<acc>` pushed AFTER reset (line 328), still surfaces as ok=false
fallback-row-malformed — len(row)!=6 → outer-else pushes `no_fallback_for_<acc>` and skips reset entirely

axes_surfaced.lines_changed: 64-67 (block expanded from 4 lines to 6 lines: 2 new elif branches)
axes_surfaced.diff_summary: added `elif (.axes_surfaced | type) == "number" then .axes_surfaced` and symmetric `elif (.axes | type) == "number" then .axes` between the array branches and the `else 0`
axes_surfaced.before_state: if a witness wrote `axes_surfaced: 9` (number), jq fall-through hit `else 0` → axes counted as 0 → witness silently classified as orphan (axes==0 is one of three orphan triggers per line 88)
axes_surfaced.after_state: scalar number form is recognized and yields the count value directly; both array and scalar forms now first-class per spec
axes_surfaced.current_corpus_impact: two witnesses use scalar form (i11_cmd_hardening=5, F23_resolution=9), but both also carry an `axes` array of matching length, so they were already being counted via the array fallback. The fix is forward-defensive: any future witness that drops the `axes` array body in favor of the lighter `axes_surfaced` scalar count will still be counted correctly instead of silently going orphan.

uniprot_live_mode.before: PASS (already worked when curl available; refs all source=live)
uniprot_live_mode.after: PASS unchanged — sentinel `__UNIPROT_BRIDGE__ PASS accession=P68871 name="Hemoglobin subunit beta" length=147 organism="Homo sapiens" refs=6 all_ok=true`

uniprot_no_fetch_mode.before: PASS (no fetch attempted, no fetch_empty_or_short pushed, fallback fills cleanly)

uniprot_fallback_engaged_mode.command: PATH=/tmp/curl_block:$PATH (with curl-stub returning empty) hexa run tool/uniprot_bridge.hexa --selftest
uniprot_fallback_engaged_mode.before: FAIL — sentinel reported `__UNIPROT_BRIDGE__ FAIL ... all_ok=false` even though fallback data was fully populated; per-ref printout would show source=fallback ok=false fails=fetch_empty_or_short — the bug surfaced in 2026-04-26_bridge_health_check.md F7
uniprot_fallback_engaged_mode.after: PASS — sentinel `__UNIPROT_BRIDGE__ PASS accession=P68871 ... refs=6 all_ok=true`; per-ref printout shows source=fallback ok=true with no fails; this is the gold-standard gaia pattern reproduced

registry_corpus_stats.before: witnesses=32 engines=3 axes=416 tier-1=106 orphans=0 duplicates=0
registry_corpus_stats.after: witnesses=32 engines=3 axes=416 tier-1=106 orphans=0 duplicates=0 (no regression — both scalar-form witnesses already had matching `axes` arrays so the array fallback was catching them; fix is forward-defensive)

tier_1_immediate_impl_this_cycle.status: applied + verified across 3 modes (live, no-fetch, fallback-engaged-via-blocked-curl)
applied + verified (32 witnesses, 0 orphans, 416 axes — no regression)

omega_stop_witness.novel_axes_ratio: 2/2 = 1.0 (both axes are direct surgical follow-ups to existing diagnostic reports — F7 in bridge_health_check + prior orphan backfill cycle — and address concrete actionable bugs)
omega_stop_witness.decision: stop — two requested fixes applied + verified; no scope creep; main thread will batch commit

raw66_error_trailer_audit.uniprot_bridge: no error trailers modified; line 360 still emits `fails              : <fails_csv>` when non-empty; sentinel FAIL branch (line 448) still emits `fails=<csv>` for genuine field-population failures
raw66_error_trailer_audit.atlas_witness_registry: no error trailers touched; jq filter is internal data extraction only; orphan-detection messaging at lines 105-110 + dashboard section unchanged

downstream_unlocks: bridge_health_check report F7 closed (uniprot moves from FAIL to PASS in fallback-engaged mode)
registry corpus is now scalar-form-tolerant — future Ω-cycle witnesses can elect the lighter `axes_surfaced: <n>` form without orphan risk
demonstrates raw 73 + raw 66 dual-constraint can be honored in tight surgical fixes (no contract breakage, no over-engineering)
